markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Build import/calls/implements/tests/documents relationships with deterministic relationship_id values. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CallImportRelationshipBuilder leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CallImportRelationshipBuilder is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: relationship_id separator-injection collision: the code-index DOES feed unescaped keys into derive_knowledge_relationship_id - file entity_key `file:{relpath}` (engine.rs:312), module entity_key `module:{import_string}` (engine.rs:620, where import_string is arbitrary JS/TS string-literal content that can contain `|` and `:`), and symbol keys `{lang}:{path}#{path}`. AT REVIEW TIME this is NEUTRALISED: the concurrently-edited storage/knowledge.rs:1145 derive_knowledge_relationship_id now uses the _v2 LENGTH-PREFIXED preimage ({byte_len}:{value} per component), which is separator-injective, and the crate compiles + 43 lib tests pass with that fix in place. My constructed collision against the old _v1 unescaped-join NO LONGER reproduces under _v2. CAVEAT: this safety is supplied by ANOTHER agent's uncommitted edit to storage/knowledge.rs (NOT part of code-index commits 5d6490b0/79e1a7e4). If that _v2 fix is reverted or not committed, the code-index's unescaped module:/file: keys reintroduce the collision. The code-index has NO defense of its own (it relies entirely on the derivation being injective).

Re-index idempotency holds for relationship_id (derivation hashes only edge_type + entity natural identities, no run/row ids) so a re-run upserts the same edge - the idempotency claim is sound. BUT the EVIDENCE SPAN for each rel is recreated every run (create_knowledge_span at engine.rs:646 always inserts a new span row keyed on content_sha256 of `{kind}|{name}|{start_byte}`); a re-index of an unchanged file accumulates duplicate edge_span rows unless span creation also dedups - the edge dedups, the span does not obviously.

Call resolution is intra-file only (resolve_symbol_by_name searches this file's symbol_index, engine.rs:593): a call to a function defined in another file produces NO references edge (silently skipped, engine.rs:599 `None => continue`). Cross-file call graph is therefore absent; the nav 'callers/callees' is file-local. Not flagged to the consumer as a limitation.
Implements edge resolves the trait by simple name within the same file (engine.rs:603); a trait imported from another module never resolves -> no implements edge. Same intra-file limitation.
evasion_probes: Re-read storage/knowledge.rs:1110-1174 (changed mid-review by the concurrent storage agent): confirmed _v2 length-prefix landed; built the crate and ran 43 code-index lib tests GREEN with _v2 in place. The _v1 collision I constructed early in the review is closed by _v2.

Verified relationships.rs is pure data (no DB), deterministic (sort+dedup at relationships.rs:98-104), conservative confidence (imports 0.95, implements 0.9, calls 0.6 because simple-name calls collide). The builder itself is sound.
Verified self-edge guard (engine.rs:640 source==target continue) and unresolved-target skip - no false/degenerate edges.

Probed simple_type stripping (relationships.rs:266): `impl Trait for module::Foo<T>` -> implements source 'Foo', target trait simple name; combined with the MT-098 impl-collision this means implements edges for two impls of the same type attach to the merged 'impl Foo' entity.
evidence_checked: runtime: cargo test --features runtime-full,test-utils --lib knowledge_code_index -> 43 passed 0 failed

hardening_pending.reason: MEDIUM(mostly auto-fixed): relationship_id separator collision shared with storage MT-054 _v2 fix; cross-file call/implements resolution absent (intra-file only - silently drops edges); evidence span recreated each re-index.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
